title: Anthropic Memory Docs
usage: Root context design, scoped rules, and low-token CLAUDE.md structure
promptSummary: Keep root CLAUDE.md concise, prefer scoped rules for subtrees, and avoid contradictory always-on instructions.

title: Anthropic Settings Docs
usage: Shared project settings, permission defaults, and deny rules for sensitive files
promptSummary: Use project settings for permissions and shared behavior; deny sensitive files instead of relying on broad ignore patterns.

title: Anthropic Subagents Docs
usage: Context isolation and parallel research guidance without moving the whole workflow into a permanent agent
promptSummary: Use subagents for isolated research and focused tasks; keep the main context small and reuse specialized prompts only when needed.

title: Get Shit Done
usage: Lean spec-driven planning, phased execution, and fresh-context task slicing
promptSummary: Spec first, discuss before planning, keep plans atomic, and avoid giant accumulated contexts by using fresh windows for execution.

title: Superpowers
usage: Triggerable skills and planning discipline instead of always-on giant prompts
promptSummary: Use compact reusable skills for planning, review, debugging, and execution instead of one broad omniprompt.

title: Everything Claude Code
usage: Token optimization, harness settings, and context hygiene patterns
promptSummary: Keep sessions cost-aware, trim root context, and route heavier workflows through scoped commands and runtime controls.

title: RTK
usage: Command-level token reduction for routine development actions
promptSummary: Reduce wasted tokens on repetitive shell workflows; treat it as an optimization layer, not a planning system.

title: Claude Mem
usage: Persistent memory retrieval patterns for later workflow expansion
promptSummary: Memory systems should retrieve compact indices first and fetch details only when necessary.

title: UI UX Pro Max
usage: UI-heavy project bootstrap with stronger design-system defaults and anti-pattern guidance
promptSummary: Pick explicit visual direction, define anti-patterns early, and avoid generic default UI choices.

title: Awesome Claude Code
usage: Discovery and follow-up research once the initial bootstrap is stable
promptSummary: Treat it as a catalog of patterns and tooling options, not a default prompt payload.

title: Ralph For Claude Code
usage: Post-spec execution automation only after the bootstrap package is approved
promptSummary: Autonomous loops belong after the spec and safety gates are in place, not in the first bootstrap step.
